name: Mindelo
summary: Mindelo is a colorful port town on São Vicente, a volcanic island in Cabo Verde, off west Africa. People speak Portuguese and Kriolu, sing soulful songs called morna, and look out across a bright blue bay at Monte Cara, a mountain shaped like a giant sleeping face.
funFacts: Mindelo's most famous singer, Cesária Évora, was called the Barefoot Diva because she always performed without shoes, just like she did as a kid.
Across the bay stands Monte Cara, a 490-metre volcanic peak whose ridges look exactly like a human face gazing up at the sky.
Every February, Mindelo throws Cape Verde's biggest Carnival, with feathered costumes, drumming bands, and a special children's parade through the streets.
Cabo Verde is ten volcanic islands that popped up from the Atlantic Ocean, and nobody lived on São Vicente at all until the 1790s.
Morna, the slow guitar music born in Mindelo, was added to UNESCO's list of world cultural treasures in 2019.
thingsToDo: Stroll ten minutes from the cruise terminal to Praça Nova, the leafy main square where local families meet under tall trees and pastel buildings.
Wander the colorful Mercado Municipal to spot tropical fruit, fresh fish, and chatty vendors selling spices in a pretty pink-and-yellow building.
Visit the Cesária Évora Museum to see the Barefoot Diva's sparkly dresses, photos, and awards inside an old colonial mansion.
Dig your toes into the soft golden sand at Praia da Laginha, a calm city beach a short walk from where the cruise ship docks.
Find the giant Cesária Évora mural and look across Porto Grande Bay for the best photo of Monte Cara's sleeping-giant face.
Catch the one-hour ferry to neighboring Santo Antão island for a quick glimpse of dramatic green mountain peaks and winding coastal roads.

localWord.meaning: A friendly Kriolu greeting meaning 'Everything all right?' that people say all day long.

images.alt: View across Porto Grande Bay in Mindelo with the Monte Cara mountain rising on the far shore.
Praça Nova, the leafy main square in the heart of Mindelo where families gather under the trees.
A bright, colourful old colonial building on a Mindelo street showing the town's Portuguese-era architecture.
Praia da Laginha, Mindelo's golden city beach with calm turquoise water perfect for a swim.
The blue-painted house of famous Cape Verdean singer Cesária Évora, a much-loved Mindelo landmark.
